3.happening: Create an empty list of course nodes.
3.why: Each course becomes a node in the dependency graph.
3.invariant: nodes will hold one entry per course.

4.why: We draw a node for each course.
4.invariant: Courses below i already have a node.

5.why: The graph needs a vertex per course.
5.invariant: Each node is labeled with its course id.

6.why: A course's in-degree is how many prerequisites it still needs.
6.invariant: indegree[c] = unmet prerequisites of course c.

7.happening: Loop over every course to prepare its in-degree.
7.why: Every course begins with a known prerequisite count.

8.why: We add to it as prerequisites are read.

9.why: Each prerequisite is a directed edge in the graph.
9.invariant: edges will hold one arrow per prerequisite.

10.why: Each pair defines a 'must come before' relationship.
10.invariant: Pairs below p are already recorded.

11.happening: Read the course that has the prerequisite (a).
11.why: a can't be taken until its prerequisite is done.

12.why: b must be finished before a.

13.why: The arrow points from prerequisite to dependent.
13.invariant: Each edge encodes one ordering constraint.

14.happening: Course {a} now has one more prerequisite.

15.happening: Start a status list for the courses.
15.why: It tracks each course: blocked, ready, or taken.

16.happening: Loop over every course to set its status.

18.why: It holds courses whose prerequisites are all met.
18.invariant: Courses are taken front to back.

19.happening: Set the queue's front marker to 0.
19.why: It points at the next course to take.
19.invariant: Courses before head are already taken.

20.happening: Loop over every course to find the ready ones.
20.why: Courses with no prerequisites can be taken immediately.

21.happening: Course {i} has no prerequisites — it's ready.
21.why: Nothing blocks it, so it can be taken first.
21.invariant: Only in-degree-0 courses seed the queue.

21.happening: Course {i} still has prerequisites — leave it blocked.
21.why: It can't be taken until they're cleared.

22.happening: Queue course {i} — it's ready to take.
22.invariant: The queue holds only ready courses.

23.invariant: A course enters the queue at most once.

24.why: No course is being taken yet.

25.why: We compare it to numCourses at the end.

26.happening: {len(queue)} course(s) are ready — take the next one.
26.why: Any ready course can be completed now.
26.invariant: head advances as courses are taken.

26.happening: No courses are ready — the process stops.
26.why: Either all courses are taken, or a cycle blocks the rest.
26.invariant: A leftover blocked course means a cycle.

27.happening: Look at the course at the front of the queue.
27.why: We take courses in the order they became ready.

28.happening: Advance the front marker (take it off the queue).
28.why: This course is being completed now.

29.happening: Remember course {node_id} as the one being taken.
29.why: The cursor highlights the active course.
29.invariant: cur points at the current course.

31.invariant: taken rises by one per completed course.

32.happening: Scan every edge to find courses that needed this one.
32.why: Completing a course can unblock its dependents.

33.happening: This edge starts at the course just taken — it unblocks a dependent.
This edge doesn't start at the current course — skip it.

34.happening: Find the dependent course at the edge's far end.
34.why: It was waiting on the course we just took.

35.why: One of its blockers is now done.

36.happening: Course {nb} has no prerequisites left — it's now ready.
36.why: Every course it depended on is taken.
Other blockers must be cleared first.

37.happening: Add the freed course {nb} to the ready-queue.

38.invariant: A course enters the queue at most once.

39.happening: Done taking this course — hide the cursor.
39.why: We move on to the next ready course.
39.invariant: cur back to -1 until the next course.

40.happening: We can finish only if all {numCourses} courses were taken.
40.why: A cycle would leave some course permanently blocked.
40.invariant: result is true exactly when taken equals numCourses.

41.why: It tells whether every course can be completed.

init.happening: Building the dependency graph and finding courses with no prerequisites.
init.why: Kahn's algorithm starts from every in-degree-0 course.
init.invariant: The queue is seeded with all ready courses.

loop.happening: Taking a ready course and scanning its dependents.
loop.why: Completing a course can unblock others.
loop.invariant: Each course is taken at most once.

check.happening: Deciding which dependents an edge unblocks, and whether one is now ready.
check.why: A course becomes ready only when all its prerequisites are done.

move.happening: Advancing — dequeuing the next ready course.
move.why: Courses are taken in the order they became ready.

update.happening: Recording a taken course and clearing prerequisites from its dependents.
update.why: State must reflect progress and newly-freed courses.

return.happening: Process complete — reporting whether all courses can be finished.
